title: Cohort Exit Strategy
description: The cohort exit strategy defines when a patient leaves the cohort after qualifying. Options include end of observation period, a fixed offset from the index date, or a drug era end / condition end event.
tips: 'End of Observation' is the default — patients stay in the cohort until they leave the database.
Use 'Fixed Duration' for analyses that require a specific follow-up window (e.g., 365 days).
Drug-era exit is useful for new-user designs where follow-up ends when treatment stops.
The persistence window ('gap days') allows short treatment gaps without ending cohort membership.
